message:
test(ft-xbnl0.2.4): watch mid-flight cancel probe confirms shared gap (tick 438)

Extends the tick 432/433/434 mid-flight-cancel-waker probes to the
fourth asupersync channel type, watch::Receiver::changed. Same test
shape (cancel-at-100-ms, select-race watcher, tolerate both Either
outcomes).

**Outcome**: watcher branch fires consistently. Confirms
watch::Receiver::changed shares the same gap as mpsc/oneshot/broadcast
— per-poll `cx.checkpoint()` short-circuit on re-poll but no
cx-cancel-waker registration for already-suspended recvs.

This generalises the finding across ALL four asupersync channel
types. The tick-437 watch module doc-comment's "likely present based
on shared design" qualifier is upgraded to "confirmed" — the doc
now cites the tick-438 test explicitly.

Cross-primitive mid-flight probe matrix complete:

| Primitive               | Pre-cancel | Mid-flight probe | Workaround test |
|-------------------------|------------|------------------|-----------------|
| mpsc::Receiver::recv    | ✓ tick 422 | ✗ tick 432       | tick 432        |
| oneshot_recv_with_cx    | ✓ tick 419 | ✗ tick 433       | tick 433        |
| broadcast_recv_with_cx  | ✓ tick 420 | ✗ tick 434       | tick 434        |
| watch::Receiver::changed| ✓ tick 423 | ✗ tick 438       | tick 438        |

Semaphore and JoinSet remain unprobed — likely same pattern but
the four-channel probe is sufficient to document the shared
asupersync design.

Observed locally: 0.11 s wall — cancel at 100 ms, watcher observes
within 50 ms poll cycle.

Check script Run 6/6 filter `watch_changed_with_cx` already catches
the new test. Run 6 grows 19 → 20. Total: 106 → 107.

Full check script passes locally via fork bypass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
